qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded entity expansion enables denial-of-service attack via resource exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable code: this finding targets unbounded entity decoding in src/justhtml/entities.py, while the advisory targets the to_markdown() <pre> serializer using a fixed backtick fence that permits Markdown fence breakout XSS.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory concerns JustHTML to_markdown() serialization of <pre> content with a fixed three-backtick fence, allowing attacker-controlled preformatted text to close the Markdown fence and expose raw HTML. The only final qualifying finding concerns unbounded entity decoding and resource consumption, not the <pre> Markdown serializer, delimiter selection, fence breakout, or downstream raw-HTML XSS condition.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-5vp3-3cg6-2rq3 describes XSS through to_markdown() serializing attacker-controlled <pre> content with a fixed backtick fence.
entity_expansion_dos/vulnerability_report.json lists code locations in src/justhtml/entities.py and tokenizer entity-decoding call sites.
entity_expansion_dos/poc_report.json reports linear parsing/resource consumption from many entity references, with no evidence of <pre> Markdown fence breakout.
